goals.description: Abandon the '20-minute stare' tactic. It's just procrastination in a fancy suit and it's making me feel worse about the scholarship deadline slipping again. I need to actually type three sentences into the document right now, even if they are terrible, to break the paralysis.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive (0.5) is screaming that the financial risk of this missed deadline outweighs the immediate comfort of avoidance. My avoidant nature wants to stay stuck safely, but the guilt is becoming too heavy to ignore. With a short planning horizon, I can't afford to let another day pass without tangible progress; facing the friction now is the only way to stop feeling like a failure.

goals.description: Skip the solo game stream for tonight. If I play while feeling guilty about the essay, it won't reset my dopamine; it'll just reinforce the narrative that I'm wasting time while the world moves on without me.
goals.rationale: I usually use low-stakes gaming as an escape valve, but today the emotional cost of 'wasting' more time on fun feels higher than usual because of the scholarship slip-up. My impulsive tendency wants instant relief, but my current stress levels make deep leisure feel risky—it might trigger a spiral where I realize I haven't written anything. Better to suffer through the writing session briefly than to compound the regret.

agent.last_reasoning: I'm finally breaking the '20-minute stare' paralysis. Typing three sentences now beats another day of guilt over this scholarship slip-up. This moves my achievement goal forward, though I feel a bit drained and should text my hallmate later so they don't think I'm ignoring them.
